{
  "analysis": "Excellent! The SSL certificate and key have been successfully generated. I can see the OpenSSL output indicating the certificate generation process completed. Now I need to create the Jupyter configuration file with all the required settings including the password hash, SSL certificate paths, port configuration, and IP binding.",
  "plan": "Now I'll create the jupyter_notebook_config.py file in the ~/.jupyter directory with all the required configuration settings: password authentication using the generated hash, SSL certificate paths, port 8888, allow connections from any IP, and allow running as root.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la ~/.jupyter/
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > ~/.jupyter/jupyter_notebook_config.py << 'EOF'
# Configuration file for jupyter-notebook.

c = get_config()

# Network and security settings
c.ServerApp.ip = '0.0.0.0'
c.ServerApp.port = 8888
c.ServerApp.open_browser = False
c.ServerApp.allow_root = True

# Password authentication
c.ServerApp.password = 'argon2:$argon2id$v=19$m=10240,t=10,p=8$522ennB8FB7qGezAogKs+A$5DzcBP3HeOoCfDaDiADeYPwp9t2k9awloLXvQZYvLb0'

# SSL/HTTPS configuration
c.ServerApp.certfile = '/root/.jupyter/jupyter.pem'
c.ServerApp.keyfile = '/root/.jupyter/jupyter.key'
EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}

{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# What is Jupyter Notebook?\n",
    "\n",
    "Jupyter Notebook is an open-source web application that allows you to create and share documents that contain live code, equations, visualizations and narrative text. Uses include: data cleaning and transformation, numerical simulation, statistical modeling, data visualization, machine learning, and much more.\n",
    "\n",
    "Key features:\n",
    "- **Interactive computing**: Execute code in real-time\n",
    "- **Rich output**: Display HTML, images, videos, LaTeX, and JavaScript\n",
    "- **Shareable**: Export notebooks to various formats (HTML, PDF, etc.)\n",
    "- **Language agnostic**: Supports over 40 programming languages"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "# Define x values from 0 to 4*pi\n",
    "x = np.linspace(0, 4*np.pi, 100)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Define sine wave\n",
    "y = np.sin(x)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create the plot\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "plt.plot(x, y, 'b-', linewidth=2, label='sin(x)')\n",
    "plt.title('Simple Sine Wave')\n",
    "plt.xlabel('x')\n",
    "plt.ylabel('sin(x)')\n",
    "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  }
 ],
